heading: Resource Requirements
body:
> **Important:** If you create a MongoDB Search index for a collection that has or will soon
> have more than 2.1 billion index objects, use the [numPartitions](fts-index-partition)
> index option to partition your index
> or [Deploy a Sharded Cluster](create-cluster-sharding). Index partitions
> are limited to 2.1 billion objects per partition.

When you [create a MongoDB Search index](ref-create-index) a MongoDB Search index, field mapping
defaults to [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings), which means that
MongoDB Search dynamically indexes all the [supported types](bson-data-chart) in your collection. Other options
such as enabling highlights can also result in your index taking up more
disk space. You can reduce the size and performance footprint of your
MongoDB Search index by:

- Specifying a custom [index definitions](ref-index-definitions) to narrow the amount and type of data that is
  indexed.

- Setting the `store` option to `false` when specifying a
  [string](bson-data-types-string) type in an index definition.

> **Note:** Some limitations apply to MongoDB Search on Free clusters and Flex clusters
> only. To learn more, see
> [MongoDB Search Search Free and Flex Tier Limitations](atlas-fts-shared-tier-limitations).
